limitations: Example report illustrates format and evidence style; it is not full live audit output.
Presence of raw SQL text(...) does not prove SQL injection exploitability.
Route inventory classification does not replace request-level authorization tests.

manual_review_required.reason: Raw SQL text(...) sites need data-flow review before any vulnerability claim.
recommended_verification: Trace request-controlled inputs into each raw SQL site.
Add injection payload tests before labeling any site vulnerable.

manual_review_required.reason: Singleton task handles are often intentional, but restart and shutdown behavior still need runtime testing.
recommended_verification: Exercise start-stop-restart flows under tests or local runs.
Confirm duplicate loop prevention and graceful cancellation behavior.

findings.title: Type-safety bypass via `as any`
findings.description: `as any` bypasses TypeScript checking at this access site. Presence is verified.
verification_steps: Replace `as any` with typed key access such as `keyof Device` where practical.
findings.false_positive_notes: Test doubles often use `as any`; this example is app code, not test code.
findings.remediation: Use a typed helper for dynamic property access instead of `as any`.

findings.title: Module-level background task singleton state
findings.description: Module-level task handle exists here. Presence is verified. Pattern is often intentional for lifecycle control.
verification_steps: Confirm matching start/stop guards exist and are covered by tests.
Review cancellation and restart behavior during shutdown/startup.
findings.false_positive_notes: Background schedulers commonly use a singleton task handle to prevent duplicate loops.
findings.remediation: Keep lifecycle guards and document task ownership semantics.

findings.title: Console `print()` call present
findings.description: Direct `print()` exists in backend runtime startup path. Presence is verified.
verification_steps: Confirm whether this code runs in production startup.
Prefer structured logging if operational visibility matters.
findings.false_positive_notes: Startup diagnostics may be intentional, but runtime code should usually use logger APIs.
findings.remediation: Replace runtime `print()` with structured logger calls.

findings.title: Exception swallowed with `pass`
findings.description: Handler drops exception with `pass` and no local logging or re-raise. Presence is verified.
verification_steps: Inspect caller behavior to confirm silent cleanup failure is acceptable.
Add logging if process-kill failure matters operationally.
findings.false_positive_notes: Best-effort cleanup may intentionally ignore kill failures.
findings.remediation: Log cleanup failures unless silent best-effort behavior is explicitly intended.

findings.title: Raw SQL `text(...)` usage present
findings.description: SQLAlchemy `text(...)` appears here. Presence is verified. Injection risk is not inferred automatically.
verification_steps: Confirm SQL is fixed literal and not built from request input.
Keep exploitability claim separate from raw SQL presence.
findings.false_positive_notes: Fixed-literal health-check SQL is commonly low risk.
findings.remediation: Document trusted-input or fixed-literal nature of raw SQL sites.
